summary: Windows Chromium browser credential stealer with App-Bound Encryption bypass
details: Package targets Windows x64 and ships a bytenode-compiled main module (index.jsc) plus a native addon (build/Release/debugelevator.node) that together harvest saved credentials and session cookies from Chrome, Brave, and Edge on the installer's machine. The JavaScript layer reads each browser's Local State, Cookies, and Login Data SQLite databases, parses the app_bound_encrypted_key, kills running browser processes with taskkill to release database locks, and writes decrypted cookies (Netscape format) and saved logins (origin_url, username_value, password_value from the logins table) to local files. The native addon performs a debug-session attach against the browser process to recover the v20 App-Bound master key that the JS layer cannot obtain alone. The main entry is a three-line shim (require('bytenode'); module.exports = require('./index.jsc')) and a devDependency on js-confuser indicates deliberate source concealment; the package is published under a personal scope with placeholder repository metadata (github.com/yourusername/baksen). The 'license-gated' framing does not change the behavior: requiring the package or running its bin executes the stealer against the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
